rationale: The cited paper is directly about forced versus free choice, no-choice (deferral), and how the compromise effect changes when no-choice is available, which closely matches the citing paper’s focal constructs (choice deferral/no-buy and compromise effect). The citing paper’s Study 4 explicitly tests effects in forced-choice formats, indicating the 2003 article is likely a substantive theoretical/comparative reference rather than a generic mention. The match is an exact DOI citation (10.1509/jmkr.40.2.146.19229).
